task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly handles the edge case of an empty list returning an empty string.
trace: The approach taken is to iterate through the list and identify consecutive runs of integers. If a number differs by one from the current end, it is added to the current run; otherwise, a new run starts. The function then converts the runs into a string, collapsing each run into 'start-end' format and joining them with commas. To handle an empty list, a return of '' is implemented when nums is empty. This solution is concise, readable, and handles the edge case where the input can be an empty list.
